mission.title: Install and Configure Slimtoolkit on Kubernetes
mission.description: SlimToolkit is a powerful tool that allows developers to optimize, inspect, and debug their container images, making them smaller and more secure without altering the original image. Installing SlimToolkit will help you enhance your container development experience significantly.

steps.title: Add the SlimToolkit Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official SlimToolkit Helm repository to your local Helm setup with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add slim https://slimtoolkit.github.io/slim
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install SlimToolkit using Helm
steps.description:
Install SlimToolkit in your Kubernetes cluster by executing the following command:
```bash
helm install slim slim/slim --namespace slim --create-namespace --version 1.40.11
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the SlimToolkit pods are running successfully with the following command:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace slim
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
To ensure optimal performance, configure resource limits for the SlimToolkit deployment:
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment/slim --namespace slim --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi
```

steps.title: Access SlimToolkit
steps.description:
You can port-forward to access the SlimToolkit service locally:
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/slim --namespace slim 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of SlimToolkit will result in the Slim pods running in the 'slim' namespace, and you will be able to access the service via port 8080 on your local machine. You can also verify that resource limits are set correctly.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add slim https://slimtoolkit.github.io/slim
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and have Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
